results.title: Chubb Data Scientist 2026 – Apply Now
highlights:
Chubb, a global insurance leader with a market presence spanning 54 countries, is expanding its AI-driven claims solutions team at its Bangalore innovation hub. This Data Scientist role is part of Chubb’s strategic push to integrate advanced analytics and large language models (LLMs) into legacy insurance workflows—a trend accelerating across the industry as firms seek to automate claims processing, reduce fraud, and improve customer experience. For freshers, this is a rare opportunity to work on real-world unstructured data (PDFs, text documents, forms) and gain exposure to cutting-edge NLP and RAG architectures, skills that are becoming table stakes in the modern data science landscape. The position is structured as a full-time internship, ideal for final-year students or recent graduates looking to build a portfolio of industry-grade projects before entering the job market.
...
| Company | Chubb |
| --- | --- |
| Role | Data Scientist |
| Location | Bangalore, Karnataka, India |
| Experience | Freshers |
| Education | Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Computer Science, Data Science, AI/ML, Statistics, or a related field |
| Salary | Not Disclosed |
...
The role is open to candidates currently pursuing or recently completing a degree in Computer Science, Data Science, AI/ML, Statistics, or a related field. Chubb places a strong emphasis on demonstrated experience over pure academics: academic, personal, or internship projects that showcase data exploration or AI/ML work will significantly strengthen your application. While a minimum GPA isn’t explicitly stated, top insurance firms like Chubb typically look for 60% or above in graduation; however, a standout project portfolio can offset average grades. If you have worked on any Kaggle competition, built a chatbot using LangChain, or analyzed messy real-world datasets, highlight that explicitly in your resume. The role requires full-time availability, so students needing to attend regular classes may need to negotiate with their institution or apply after completing coursework.
...
- Work on AI-driven claims solutions to automate and improve insurance claim handling.
...
- Explore and mine large, unstructured claims datasets to surface actionable patterns and insights.
- Break down complex, ambiguous business problems into well-scoped, executable technical tasks.
...
- Document findings, code, and
...
- Python (data manipulation, scripting, automation)
- Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Seaborn, Plotly
- Machine learning concepts (scikit-learn)
- NLP and text processing techniques
- Problem-solving and analytical thinking
- LLMs, prompt engineering, RAG architectures (good to have)
- LangChain, Azure OpenAI (good to have)
- Unstructured data processing (PDFs, text, forms) (good to have)
...
To maximize your chances, focus on three layers. First, solidify your Python—not just syntax but data manipulation with Pandas and visualization with Matplotlib/Seaborn. Second, get comfortable with scikit-learn: train a classifier on the Iris dataset or a regression on Boston housing (despite its ethics issues, it’s still widely used for learning). Third, start experimenting with LLMs: use OpenAI’s API or free alternatives like Hugging Face models to build a simple Q&A system over a PDF. Chubb explicitly asks for ‘exposure to LLMs, prompt engineering, or RAG architectures’—this is the edge that most applicants lack. Spend a weekend following a LangChain tutorial and deploying a basic RAG pipeline on Streamlit. Document it on GitHub.
...
Candidates currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Computer Science, Data Science, AI/ML, Statistics, or a related field are eligible. Academic or personal projects demonstrating data exploration or AI/ML experience are a strong advantage. The role is a full-time internship, so you must be available for a standard work schedule.

Director, AI Engineering (AVP) at [Chubb](https://www.linkedin.com/company/chubb)
...
Seasoned AI/ML Engineering leader with extensive experience in building machine learning solutions, platforms, and large-scale deployments. My career has been shaped by a wide range of startup environments—from scrappy early-stage teams to growth-stage scale-ups, and a company navigating acquisition where I’ve learned how to lay strong foundations, scale systems, and create lasting value through ML and data. At Chubb, I lead AI engineering for enterprise LLM applications, driving system and service design, deployment strategy, and observability for agentic AI solutions. What I bring: • ML & GenAI Development — Production-ready applications that drive measurable outcomes. • ML Platforms & MLOps — Scalable pipelines, LLM-first workflows, and data platforms serving millions. • System & Service Design for LLMs — Distributed architectures, low-latency retrieval, vector DB & embedding infra, autoscaling inference, API/gateway design, observability, and reliability. • Startup Agility & Scaling — From scrappy early-stage teams to post-acquisition transitions, I navigate trade-offs and deliver results. • Engineering Leadership — Building high-performing teams and aligning technical vision with business goals. I thrive at the intersection of hands-on technical depth, system-level architecture, and strategic leadership—helping organizations unlock the full potential of AI/ML, whether it’s building from scratch, scaling rapidly, or integrating through acquisition. ### Gen AI- CPU LLM Explorer & Document Assistant (June 2025)
...
Youtube link for demo: https://youtu.be/7O6oAchsPR8 Git code: https://github.com/prtmoswal/cpu_llm_explorer_document_assistant_genAI Developed an interactive Streamlit application enabling local execution and exploration of small Large Language Models (LLMs) like distilgpt2 and tinystories entirely on CPU, ensuring offline functionality. • Implemented Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) capabilities, allowing users to upload and process custom .docx documents to provide dynamic context for LLM responses, enhancing factual accuracy and domain-specific generation. • Designed a user-friendly interface with features for model selection, prompt input, and adjustable generation parameters (e.g., max_new_tokens, temperature). • Explored the practical challenges and limitations of deploying larger quantized models (e.g., Phi-2, Gemma-2B) on local CPU environments. • Demonstrated proficiency in building end-to-end Generative AI applications with a focus on resource efficiency and accessibility for study and experimentation. • Technologies Used: • Python: Core programming language. • Streamlit: For interactive web application development. • Hugging Face Transformers/Diffusers: For integrating pre-trained LLMs. • FAISS: For efficient similarity search in RAG implementation (implied by faiss_index). • python-docx: For document processing. • os, tempfile: For file handling. results.title: Raj Mukherjee
highlights:
CDAO | GenAI Advisor | Head of Data,Analytics and AI EMEA@Chubb
...
Head of Advanced Analytics and AI, EMEA at [Chubb](https://www.linkedin.com/company/chubb)
...
As the Head of Advanced Analytics and AI at Chubb EMEA, I lead the strategic development and implementation of advanced analytics and artificial intelligence solutions across one of the world's largest property and casualty insurance organizations. With nearly two decades of experience in data and analytics leadership, I am focused on transforming underwriting and operational processes through innovative AI solutions, portfolio optimization, and data-driven decision making. Having recently joined Chubb from Direct Line Group, where I successfully led data and analytics transformation initiatives, I bring a wealth of experience in leveraging analytics and AI to drive business value across the insurance value chain. My expertise spans predictive modeling, machine learning, data engineering, and AI implementation, with a proven track record of translating complex analytical solutions into tangible business outcomes. In my new role, I am passionate about building upon our analytical capabilities to enhance underwriting excellence and operational efficiency. I am committed to fostering collaboration between analytics, underwriting, and operations teams to enhance our decision-making processes and drive growth in both traditional and emerging markets. I believe in the power of data and AI to transform the insurance industry while maintaining the human expertise that has always been central to our business. My goal is to help drive innovation through advanced analytics and AI capabilities to enhance our competitive advantage, improve customer experience, and drive sustainable growth across our diverse portfolio. Always eager to learn and collaborate, I am excited to work with talented teams across our global network to shape the future of insurance through innovative analytical solutions and AI-driven transformation.
...

My expertise spans predictive modeling, machine learning, data engineering, and AI implementation, with a proven track record of translating complex analytical solutions into tangible business outcomes. In my new role, I am passionate about building upon our analytical capabilities to enhance underwriting excellence and operational efficiency.
